summary: Joel Spolsky announces an upcoming speaking engagement at Web.Start in Zagreb, Croatia. He also issues a correction and apology for previously referring to the United Kingdom as 'England' in a data analysis post. He explains the mix-up came from their order form dropdown listing, and offers to buy Edinburgh readers a pint when he visits on May 19th.
key_points: Promoting his upcoming talk at Web.Start conference in Zagreb, Croatia on May 17th
Correcting a previous post where he mistakenly referred to the UK as 'England'
Using humor and self-deprecation to acknowledge the geographic gaffe, with playful links to the Falkland Islands and Disney World's UK pavilion
money_quotes: Inadvertently, I referred to the UK as 'England.'
By way of apologies, if you're in Edinburgh when I pass through there on May 19th, let me buy you a pint.
key_insight: A lighthearted travel announcement and correction post, notable mainly for Spolsky's characteristic self-deprecating humor in handling a geographic faux pas about the UK.
